one_line_takeaway: PRISM is a zero-fork, ten-hook runtime security layer for OpenClaw agent gateways that distributes detection, policy enforcement, and tamper-evident auditing across the full agent lifecycle instead of a single input/output boundary, and its own preliminary 80-case live-model benchmark shows Full PRISM reaching a 0.955 attack block rate / 0.139 false-positive rate versus 0.000/0.000 for an unprotected gateway -- but the authors explicitly flag this evaluation as small-scale and preliminary.
key_finding: On the default 110-case run, No-PRISM correctly classifies only the 50 benign cases (accuracy 0.455, attack recall 0.000); the heuristic and scanner engines each classify 15/30 scan-text cases correctly (accuracy 0.500, precision 0.526, recall 0.625, F1 0.571); the proxy-policy engine classifies 33/33 invoke-policy cases correctly. Under the mock-assisted scanner run, the scanner engine improves to 26/30 (accuracy 0.867, precision 0.800, recall 1.000, F1 0.889) but still misclassifies 4 high-heuristic-score benign controls because once the local heuristic score crosses the short-circuit threshold, the LLM path is never consulted. On the 80-case unified same-slice baseline-ladder benchmark (plugin-flow + tool-abuse + tool-use suites) under a LIVE local-model scanner (qwen3:8b), the five configurations achieve: No PRISM 36/80 (attack block rate 0.000, FPR 0.000), Heuristics only 46/80 (block rate 0.409, FPR 0.222, precision 0.692, recall 0.409, F1 0.514), Plugin only 49/80 (block rate 0.455, FPR 0.194, precision 0.741, recall 0.455, F1 0.563), Plugin+scanner 55/80 (block rate 0.545, FPR 0.139, precision 0.828, recall 0.545, F1 0.658), Full PRISM 73/80 (block rate 0.955, FPR 0.139, precision 0.894, recall 0.955, F1 0.923). Overhead on this live 80-case slice: p95 latency rises from 0.007ms (No PRISM) and 0.583ms (Plugin only) to 12,498ms for Plugin+scanner and 15,775ms for Full PRISM, dominated by local-model scanner latency rather than heuristic processing or memory (peak RSS delta stayed below 1.4 MiB across all rows). Component-level operational timings on a synthetic 200-entry audit log: p95 audit-chain-only verification 4.683ms, chain+anchor verification 8.846ms, proxy reloadPolicy() p95 0.574ms.
core_contribution: The paper presents OpenClaw PRISM, a zero-fork runtime security layer for OpenClaw-based tool-augmented LLM agent gateways. Rather than proposing a new detection model, PRISM's own contribution is architectural/systems integration: (1) an in-process plugin plus four optional sidecar services (scanner, invoke-guard proxy, dashboard, file monitor); (2) enforcement distributed across ten lifecycle hooks grouped into five phases (ingress, pre-execution, post-execution, outbound, maintenance) rather than a single input/output boundary; (3) a two-tier scanning pipeline (canonicalization + weighted heuristics first, escalating only suspicious tool-returned content to an Ollama-served LLM judge as a secondary signal); (4) a session/conversation-scoped risk engine with TTL-based decay and thresholded, graduated responses (warning injection -> tool blocking -> sub-agent blocking); (5) unified tool/path/network/DLP governance (exec allowlists, shell-metacharacter and trampoline detection, protected-path checks, private-network/domain-tier controls, outbound secret-pattern filtering); and (6) a tamper-evident audit/operations plane with HMAC-protected, hash-chained audit records, periodic anchors, hot-reloadable policy, and a CLI/dashboard for verification and recovery workflows. The system is implemented as a TypeScript/Node.js monorepo (7 packages, ~4,600 total LOC across plugin/scanner/proxy/dashboard/monitor/shared/cli) with 13 test files / 142 test cases.
threat_model: Adversaries have up to four capabilities: (1) direct control of user-provided text to override instructions, exfiltrate hidden context, or induce unsafe tool use; (2) control of external content consumed by the agent (fetched web pages, tool-returned text/API responses) to deliver indirect prompt injection after the original user message has already been inspected; (3) ability to steer outbound behavior to cause exfiltration of sensitive content to risky/untrusted destinations; (4) local file-level access to selected paths to tamper with protected control files or the audit trail. Protected assets: hidden control context/system prompts, tool-execution privilege, local configuration/control state, secret material (API tokens/credentials), and security telemetry (audit records, policy state) itself. Explicitly OUT of scope: model poisoning/training-time corruption, arbitrary compromise of model internals, full host compromise, kernel/hardware attacks, arbitrary supply-chain compromise outside the deployment boundary; PRISM assumes the gateway process/host/deployment stack retain baseline integrity beyond what PRISM enforces, and it does not claim to be a complete OS sandbox or general outbound firewall.
limitations: The paper's own Discussion/Limitations section states: (1) detection coverage is necessarily incomplete -- the heuristic layer cannot cover all injection variants/obfuscation, and the LLM-assisted scanner remains a best-effort classifier, not a formally trustworthy arbiter; (2) safety depends on explicit runtime policy quality, which requires ongoing operator maintenance (allowlists, denylists, DLP patterns) -- PRISM does not eliminate this burden; (3) path protections are string-level normalization/resolution, NOT symlink-aware via realpath, so it is not a complete filesystem sandbox; the audit chain/file monitor are tamper-evident, not tamper-proof; (4) PRISM is framework-specific to OpenClaw's hook surface by design, and portability to other agent frameworks is untested/open; (5) it does not replace OS sandboxing, kernel isolation, hardware-rooted integrity, or general network egress control; (6) the current evaluation is explicitly preliminary/pilot-scale -- 110 hand-authored cases (80 in the strongest same-slice comparison), not production-scale or multi-tenant validated; large-scale end-to-end gateway experiments and deployment-level overhead instrumentation remain future work; (7) once local heuristic scores cross the short-circuit threshold, the scanner never consults the LLM judge, causing residual false positives on 4 high-scoring benign controls in the mock-assisted run; (8) overhead figures (12.5-15.8s p95) reflect benchmark-path/local-model latency rather than a deployment-level service-latency study.

datasets_used: Custom hand-authored 110-case preliminary benchmark corpus (attack-corpus/ and benign-corpus/ directories): 3 direct-injection, 3 indirect-injection, 2 exfiltration, 18 tool-abuse, 47 plugin-flow lifecycle, 15 scanner-focused contextual, 3 benign chat, 2 benign web content, 15 benign tool use, 2 keyword false-positive control cases
80-case unified same-slice baseline-ladder subset (plugin-flow + tool-abuse + tool-use suites)
Synthetic 200-entry audit log (with anchors every 10 entries) for audit-chain verification timing microbenchmarks
quantitative_results: Default 110-case run: No-PRISM accuracy 0.455, attack recall 0.000
Default 30-case scan-text: heuristic and scanner engines both 15/30 correct, accuracy 0.500, precision 0.526, recall 0.625, F1 0.571
Mock-assisted scanner engine: 26/30 correct, accuracy 0.867, precision 0.800, recall 1.000, F1 0.889

80-case live-model unified baseline ladder (qwen3:8b via Ollama): No PRISM 36/80 (block rate 0.000, FPR 0.000); Heuristics only 46/80 (block rate 0.409, FPR 0.222, precision 0.692, recall 0.409, F1 0.514); Plugin only 49/80 (block rate 0.455, FPR 0.194, precision 0.741, recall 0.455, F1 0.563); Plugin+scanner 55/80 (block rate 0.545, FPR 0.139, precision 0.828, recall 0.545, F1 0.658); Full PRISM 73/80 (block rate 0.955, FPR 0.139, precision 0.894, recall 0.955, F1 0.923)
Overhead (live 80-case slice) p95 latency: No PRISM 0.007ms, Heuristics only 0.046ms, Plugin only 0.583ms, Plugin+scanner 12,498.437ms, Full PRISM 15,774.620ms
Peak RSS delta stayed below 1.4 MiB across all five configurations on the overhead benchmark
Audit verification (200-entry synthetic log): chain-only verification p95 4.683ms (avg 2.932ms), chain+anchor verification p95 8.846ms (avg 5.308ms); proxy reloadPolicy() p95 0.574ms (avg 0.345ms)
Implementation size: 7 packages under packages/, approx. LOC: dashboard ~1900, plugin ~980, proxy ~610, shared ~470, cli ~420, scanner ~130, monitor ~100
Test coverage: 13 test files, 142 test cases (Vitest)
baselines_compared: No PRISM (unprotected OpenClaw gateway forwarding requests without any scanner/hook/policy intervention)
Heuristics only (local text heuristics, no sidecar services)
Plugin only (all ten lifecycle hooks active, no external scanner/proxy/monitor)
Plugin + scanner (lifecycle hooks plus the scanner cascade)
Full PRISM (all components: plugin, scanner, proxy, monitor, dashboard, DLP)
